expressions.english: things (to do) which you hate; job you hate
expressions.notes: 일 = work; task; thing, stuff; occasion

expressions.english: a subject you hate
a person you hate
to hate bugs
to hate studying
I hate shopping.
expressions.notes:
전 = (polite) I (subject)
전 is the contracted form of 저는.
쇼핑하다 = to do one's shopping, go shop

expressions.english: Sometimes you also have to do things you hate.
expressions.notes:
가끔 = sometimes
-이/어/아이어 되다 = to have to + V

expressions.english: I hate (to do) this, but I have to do it.
expressions.notes:
-지만 = but, however; though
꼭 = for sure; at any cost; certainly; definitely; make sure to (do something); tight
-이/어/아이어 하다 = should; have to; must

expressions.korean: 그 사람이 저 싫어하는 거 같아요.
expressions.english: I think he hates me.
My son really hates studying.
He's the type (of person) who hates to lose.
expressions.notes:
지다 = to lose
성격 = personality, disposition

expressions.korean: 왜 자꾸 제가 싫어하는 행동을 하세요?
expressions.english: Why do you keep doing things I hate?
expressions.notes:
왜 = why; for what reason; how come
자꾸 = repeatedly, again and again
행동 = behavior

expressions.english: I don't hate it, but I don't like it either.
expressions.notes:
아니다 = to be not
좋아하다 = to like

expressions.korean: 저는 집안일 중에 설거지를 제일 싫어 해요.
expressions.english: Among all the household chores, I hate washing dishes the most.

expressions.korean: 이 세상에 휴기를 싫어하는 사람은 없 어요.
expressions.english: There is no one in this world who hates vacation.

expressions.korean: 저는 우리 반에 싫어하는 사람이 한 명 도 없어요.
expressions.english: There isn't anyone in the class I hate.
expressions.notes:
우리 = we; us
반 = class
명 = counter for people

expressions.korean: 제가 싫어하는 거 알면서 왜 자꾸 담배 를 피워요?
expressions.english: You know I hate it, so why do you still keep smoking?
expressions.notes:
(으)면서 = while + S + V-ing
왜 = why; for what reason; how come
자꾸 = repeatedly, again and again
담배를 피우다 = to smoke

expressions.korean: 저희 부모님은 제가 거짓말하는 걸 정 말 싫어하세요.
expressions.english:
My parents really hate me lying to them.
My parents really hate it when I lie.
expressions.notes:
저희 = (polite) we; us; our
부모님 = parents
거짓말하다 = to lie

expressions.korean: 저는 좋아하는 과목은 잘하는데 싫어하 는 과목은 잘 못해요.
expressions.english: I'm good at the subjects I like, but I'm bad at the subjects I hate.
expressions.notes: 못하다 = to be bad at something, poor at something

expressions.korean: 높은 곳에서 갑자기 떨어지는 느낌을 싫어해서 놀이기구를 안 타요.
expressions.english: I really hate the feeling of suddenly dropping from a high place, so I don't go on amusement park rides.
expressions.notes:
높다 = to be high; tall
높은 is the adjective form of 높다.
떨어지다 = to fall, drop (down from somewhere), plummet
느낌 = feeling, sense
놀이기구 = amusement park rides
타다 = to ride; to get on; to take (vehicle)
